summary: Baer acknowledges Szarfman knows more about data mining but characterizes her comments as atypical.
body: Responding to Zinderman, Baer acknowledges that "[Szarfman] knows a lot more about data mining than I do[.]"[^fn-109] However, Baer reiterates her earlier concerns about Szarfman's apparent comments during a regularly scheduled call between officials at CBER, CDER, and Commonwealth.[^fn-110] Baer characterizes Szarfman's comments as atypical and speculates that Szarfman may have been "just brainstorming and theorizing," which was "unusual compared to the typical topics covered on the call."[^fn-111]
